title: RFE: Command to alias to docker.sock without requiring podman-machine reboot
body:
### Feature request description

# User Story

This RFE relates to the following user story:

**"As a user on Mac or Windows using Podman Desktop, I would like to enable Docker compatibility mode on my Podman machine so third-party tools with Docker support will run using Podman."**

# Context

We are working on the design of improved compose support in Podman Desktop. There's a little bit of awkwardness in the user experience that is possible right now. Let me explain. This would be the workflow right now if we implemented it today (posting work-in-progress wireframe for visual):

![Screenshot from 2023-07-01 14-53-14](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/799683/cda6b2b5-a5e4-4670-8653-8d36c700a496)

## Workflow possible w/o RFE

### Assumptions

- User is running Mac or Windows
- User has Podman Desktop installed
- User has created a podman machine, likely via the Podman Desktop UI

### Steps

1. I have a podman machine, and it is running.
2. I go to the wireframed screen above, and I turn on the "Enabled" switch for "Third-party Docker tool compatibility" (AKA "Docker compatibility") 
3. I get a little alert that I woń t have Docker compatibility until I restart the Podman machine OR I am warned that the Podman machine will need to restart and confirm and it is done for me. [I think it would be too rude to just go ahead and restart the podman machine without a heads up - they may having something running on it that would be impacted?]
4. I restart the podman machine, great, I have docker compat running, it works, happy days. 
5. Now I want to turn off Docker compat because I need to run some things using the docker engine. I click the switch to disable it. Yet again, I am prompted to restart the podman machine to apply the change. 

## Problem Statement

It is a bit heavy to require rebooting the podman VM in order to apply the Docker compatibility feature / docker socket alias be applied. Right now, it is required because the alias is set up when podman machine starts up. 


### Suggest potential solution


## RFE / The Ask

Would it be possible to add a dedicated command to update the status of the docker.sock alias, so that it could be done more quickly / without additional user intervention and without requiring a VM reboot? Something like

`podman machine alias-socket-to-var-run-docker.sock`

This would allow for a much simpler user flow in Podman Desktop's UI, and would leave the user less likely to be stuck in a confusing state (e.g. "I thought I enabled docker compatibility, why is my tool not running saying it can't find docker?" because they didn't realize / missed the memo the vm had to be rebooted.) 

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The alternative is what is mocked up. But I suspect we could do better for the users with the RFE, it would simplify things for them.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
Can you elaborate why Podman Desktop needs to restart the machine?  What can `podman machine` do that Desktop cannot that wouldn't require a restart?

Is is the "compat mode" still needed once the compose idea is implemented?

>Why Podman Desktop needs to restart the machine

Podman Desktop don't need that directly. It's just that `/var/run/docker.sock` aliasing (like podman-mac-helper) is called/added only when a podman machine start

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/03d7ed468dfcaa883d164382b60306d131e8aa91/pkg/machine/qemu/machine.go#L1092

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/03d7ed468dfcaa883d164382b60306d131e8aa91/pkg/machine/qemu/machine.go#L1106

but for example it's just doing a symlink
```
ls -la /var/run/docker.sock                                                                                                                                                                                                  
lrwxr-xr-x@ 1 root  daemon  65 Jul  3 08:49 /var/run/docker.sock -> /Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman.sock
```

this is why this command could be called at another time (user trigerring) instead of when the machine starts.

And when the user want to enable/disable, now we need to restart the machine as it's only at that time that podman do the symlink. While Podman Desktop could just ask podman to do it when requested.


> Is is the "compat mode" still needed once the compose idea is implemented?

yes
Why ?
Compat mode is to enable 3rd party tools that use hardcoded `/var/run/docker.sock` (on macOS for example) as a way to detect/use container engines.

Thanks, @benoitf!  I still have some knowledge gaps on the machine side.

To me, it feels surprising that machine is doing some of the symlinking in the first place.  Do you know why it's doing that?

My naive take at the moment is that machine only takes care of podman and its socket and not touch docker at all.  This way, Desktop and other users can do the symlinking and management etc. when needed.

machine is doing that as people expect to have a working environment when they start a machine. (and some don't rely on/use Podman Desktop)

> My naive take at the moment is that machine only takes care of podman and its socket and not touch docker at all. This way, Desktop and other users can do the symlinking and management etc. when needed.

podman-mac-helper is a root service (because of course `/var/run` is only accessible by root)

I think that as having it part of the 'start' sequence is a real advantage, just that it could be prompted at another time.
Seems `podman machine set` should be able to grow this functionality.

Before starting a PR, let's first iron a design please.  I don't feel qualified to judge since I have knowledge gaps wrt. Podman on Macs.

@ashley-cui @baude PTAL
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vyasgun @vrothberg @ashley-cui What is going on with this Issue?
I still feel unqualified to decide what's best.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
